title: brain doctor — --json success output
description: Read-only host/vault health diagnostic (pure — no ledger row; available even when the backup watermark is unhealthy). Runs the Phase-1 check inventory (x-atlas-contract.checkInventory): modes/permissions, lock liveness (+ --reclaim-locks for dead-pid locks), backup watermark health, and the audit-head anchor check. `--reclaim-locks` is the one narrow mutation: it removes lock records whose owning pid is provably dead. Aggregate exit is 0 when all checks pass, 6 when any check reports an operator action is required.

reclaimedLocks.description: Present only when `--reclaim-locks` was passed: the dead-pid lock records reclaimed.

checks.title: Lock liveness
checks.detail: vault-maintenance held by dead pid 44122 — run --reclaim-locks

x-atlas-contract.summary: Host/vault health checks; --reclaim-locks reclaims dead-pid locks.

flags.description: Reclaim lock records whose owning pid is provably dead. The only mutation this command performs.

sideEffects: with --reclaim-locks: removes lock records held by a dead pid (no ledger row; lock-file maintenance only)
prohibitedEffects: no vault, projection, or backup write

errorCodes.when: the configured vault/repo is missing or unreadable
unexpected internal failure while running a check

checkInventory.title: Filesystem modes & permissions
checkInventory.description: Verify the protected-ref permission layout and key ACLs match the security/broker contract (ref dirs 0750 / files 0640 owned atlas-broker:atlas-git; refs/agent 0770 agent-owned; WORM anchor 0600). Reports action-required on any drift.

checkInventory.title: Lock liveness
checkInventory.description: Inspect every named lock scope (vault-maintenance ⊐ ledger-maintenance ⊐ jobs-runner ⊐ canonical-integration) for a live holder. A lock whose owning pid is dead is reported action-required and is reclaimable via --reclaim-locks.

checkInventory.title: Backup watermark health
checkInventory.description: Compare backup_watermark.seq against the latest ledger seq. degraded/action-required when the watermark lags (the backup-unhealthy blocking state); healthy when fully covered.

checkInventory.title: Audit-head anchor check
checkInventory.description: Verify the refs/audit/runs head and monotonic event count against the external WORM anchor (D8). action-required on count regression or head mismatch (anti-truncation).

checkInventory.title: Quarantine store security
checkInventory.description: Validate the encrypted-quarantine store's at-rest posture (Task 2.2 / #28): the resolved dir is outside the repo+vault and (when present) mode 0700 with no symlink or stray non-bundle files, and the `quarantine-aead` key ACL posture (readableBy ['trusted-cli'], parserModelDenied, identity 'agent', file 'quarantine-aead.key', mode 0600) plus the atlas-egress group exclusion (D18) match the security contract. action-required on any drift, invalid/unreadable dir, or (on a provisioned host) a missing ACL matrix; degraded on crash-leftover temps.

checkInventory.title: Sandbox capability probe
checkInventory.description: Surface @atlas/sources' probeSandbox() — the per-host isolation matrix of sandbox-contract.md (Task 2.3 / #29): no-network, empty-environment, isolated-filesystem, no-credential-access, no-inherited-fds, no-subprocess, syscall-restriction, resource-caps, scan-before-persist. ok when every REQUIRED guarantee's primitive is available on this host; action-required (fail loud) when any is missing — the parser worker then refuses to launch (fail closed) so no untrusted input is parsed. Guards against macOS Seatbelt / Linux bwrap+seccomp version churn removing a primitive.
